name: Tirana Digital Hub
description: A sleek co-living studio in Blloku with 500Mbps fibre, a sit-stand desk, and a rooftop terrace. Built for remote workers who want caffeine, bandwidth, and a city that doesn't sleep.

name: Blloku Boutique Apartment
description: A design-forward two-room apartment on the former Communist Party bloc, now Tirana's most fashionable neighbourhood. Ten minutes on foot to every restaurant worth going to.

name: Tirana Sky Penthouse
description: A full-floor penthouse with panoramic city views, rooftop plunge pool, and a private chef available on request. The most unapologetically luxurious stay in the capital.

name: Durrës Beachfront Studio
description: Step off the terrace directly onto the Adriatic beach. Simple, clean, and 35 minutes from Tirana airport. Best value on the coast for a couples' escape.

name: Saranda Bay Retreat
description: A bright three-bedroom villa perched above Saranda's crescent bay. Private pool, sunbeds, a gated garden, and a fully equipped kitchen for family meals. Corfu is visible from the terrace.

name: Ksamil Paradise Bungalow
description: A whitewashed bungalow 40 metres from the turquoise water of Ksamil's famous islets. The beach is practically your front garden.

name: Vlorë Cliffside Apartment
description: Perched on the limestone cliffs south of Vlorë with sweeping views of the Bay of Vlorë and the Karaburun peninsula. A dramatic base for exploring the Riviera.

name: Himara Riviera Cottage
description: An old stone cottage tucked into the hillside above Himara. Fig trees in the garden, the sound of cicadas at night, and a fifteen-minute walk to a nearly empty beach.

name: Dhermi Beach House
description: A spacious six-person house on Albania's most celebrated beach. White pebbles, electric-blue water, and a party scene for those who want it — or total solitude 200 metres down the coast.

name: Berat Stone Guesthouse
description: Inside the walls of Berat's Ottoman old town — a UNESCO World Heritage site — this 200-year-old stone house has original carved ceilings, a terrace overlooking the Osum valley, and a host who makes the best byrek in the city.

name: Gjirokastër Ottoman Mansion
description: A restored 18th-century kulla (tower house) in Gjirokastër's stone city, another UNESCO site. Vaulted ceilings, bay windows with mountain views, antique furnishings, and the feeling that time has stopped.

name: Shkodra Lakeside B&B
description: A family-run B&B on the shores of Lake Shkodër — the largest lake in the Balkans. Kayaks included, homemade rakia at sundown, and a cycling path that goes all the way to the Accursed Mountains.

name: Pogradec Ohrid View
description: A tranquil lakeside apartment in Pogradec with floor-to-ceiling windows over Lake Ohrid. Shared garden, freshwater swimming, and the best koran fish you will ever eat from the restaurant next door.

name: Valbona Valley Chalet
description: A wooden chalet in the heart of the Accursed Mountains national park. The trailhead for the famous Valbona–Theth hike starts at the garden gate. Zero phone signal. Maximum mountains.

name: Tirana Art District Loft
description: A high-ceilinged loft in Tirana's emerging creative quarter, surrounded by galleries, independent coffee shops, and street murals. The host is an artist who leaves a different painting in the apartment each month.
